data.title: Lindt completes buyback early, launches new program in May
data.description:
• Swiss chocolate maker Lindt (LDSVF) completed its buyback program, which started on August 2, 2024, ahead of schedule on April 9, 2026. The company said the new buyback program will start in May 2026. 
  • As announced on March 10, 2026, Chocoladefabriken Lindt & Sprüngli AG decided to launch a new buyback program for registered shares and participation certificates of up to CHF 1 billion, which will run for up to three years.
  • Due to the completion of the existing buyback program ahead of schedule, the new buyback program will already start in May 2026. Separate trading lines will be opened on SIX Swiss Exchange for the new buyback program of registered shares and participation certificates.

data.title: European markets subdued as oil surge clouds outlook
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.15% to 10,618

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.18% to 23,834. Germany's inflation touches highest level since January 2024

France (CAC:IND) +0.08% to 8,252.

In other parts of Europe, the Czech unemployment rate fell to 5% in March.

Switzerland’s consumer sentiment index fell sharply to -43 in March.

Norway’s annual inflation rate accelerated to 3.6% in March.

Industrial production in Slovakia dropped by 2.9% Y/Y in February.

Industrial production in Sweden rose by 7.0% Y/Y in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.29% higher to 614.4, supported by optimism over a U.S.-Iran ceasefire that could potentially lead to a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. However, crude oil prices surged toward $100 mark as geopolitical tensions continued to dominate market direction. The prospects of improved shipping conditions have bolstered risk sentiment across global markets. 

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up less than 1 basis point to 4.30%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points to 4.80%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 3.02%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: iQIYI eyes theme park in Wuhan, slated for 2027
data.description:
• China’s streaming platform iQIYI (IQ) plans to develop its first theme park in Central China, marking its first project integrated into an urban renewal initiative and physical entertainment experiences.
  • The company announced plans to build iQIYI LAND in Wuhan, with the project slated to open in 2027.
  • The park will be located within the Hanyangzao Cultural and Creative Industry Park and is positioned to create a new cultural tourism landmark.
  • The park will feature immersive theaters, live performances, film and TV scene recreations, character interactions, and social gaming experiences, all built around iQIYI’s portfolio of Chinese dramas, animation, and variety content.
  • The Wuhan project builds on the early success of iQIYI LAND Yangzhou, which opened in February 2026 as the company’s first immersive theme park and has received strong user ratings on local platforms.

data.title: Intel, Google team up for AI and cloud infrastructure
data.description:
Intel (INTC) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google entered a multiyear collaboration to advance the next generation of AI and cloud infrastructure with Xeon CPUs and custom infrastructure processing units, or IPUs.

Under the collaboration, the two companies will align across multiple generations of Intel's Xeon processors to improve performance, energy efficiency, and total cost of ownership across Google’s global infrastructure.

The companies said they are expanding their co-development of custom application-specific integrated circuit, or ASIC-based IPUs. These programmable accelerators offload networking, storage, and security functions from host CPUs, thereby increasing efficiency and enabling more predictable performance across hyperscale AI environments, according to the companies.

"CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand," said Intel's CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

data.title: Agree Realty raises monthly dividend by 1.9% to $0.267 a share
data.description:
• Agree Realty (ADC) declares $0.267/share monthly dividend, 1.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.262.
  • Forward yield 4.11%
  • Payable May 14; for shareholders of record April 30; ex-div April 30.

  • See ADC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: White House warns staff against placing bets on Polymarket, Kalshi
data.description:
The White House warned its staff against using insider information to place bets on prediction markets like Polymarket (POLYMARKET) and Kalshi (KALSHI), according to multiple media reports, citing officials.

"Recent press reports have raised concerns about government officials using nonpublic government information to place wagers on online prediction markets," the White House Management Office wrote in a staff-wide email last month. 

"All White House employees are reminded that the misuse of nonpublic information by government employees for financial benefit is a very serious offense and will not be tolerated," it added, noting that buying or selling prediction market contracts using classified information was a criminal offense.

The email was sent a day after President Donald Trump announced on social media a five-day pause on strikes against Iran.

Around 15 minutes before his announcement, a flurry of activity kicked off in the futures markets. Over $760M worth of oil futures contracts changed hands in under two minutes, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing Dow Jones Market Data.

Three accounts on Polymarket earned over $600,000 by betting on the timing of this week's ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

"President Trump has been crystal clear: while he seeks a strong and profitable stock market for everyone, members of Congress and other government officials should be prohibited from using nonpublic information for financial benefit," White House spokesman Davis Ingle said.

"Any implication that administration officials are engaged in such activity without evidence is baseless and irresponsible reporting," he added.

There is no evidence that anyone within the Trump administration is using inside information to place well-timed bets on prediction markets.

data.title: Richelieu Hardware GAAP EPS of $0.26, revenue of $463.6M
data.description:
• Richelieu Hardware press release (RHUHF): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.26.
  • Revenue of $463.6M (+5.0% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted cash flows from operating activities of $37.9 million.
  • EBITDA of $43.2 million, up 1.9 % - EBITDA margin of 9.3 %.
  • Strong financial position as at February 28, 2026 - working capital of $625.7 million - ratio of 3.2:1.
  • Quarterly dividend of $0.1566 per share payable on May 7, 2026 to shareholders of record on April 23, 2026.

data.title: ASE Technology reports 33% jump in ATM business revenue; March sales surge 19% Y/Y
data.description:
ASE Technology (ASX) said that its March revenue rose 19% Y/Y to ~$1.95B, up 17.9% M/M.

For Q1, revenues were up 21.9% year-over-year to $5.51 billion, down 4.4% sequentially.

Revenue in its home currency was up 14.6% Y/Y and 18.2% sequentially, to NT$ 61.58B.

Net revenues for ATM assembly, testing, and material business were $1.26M, up 32.6% Y/Y and 13.6% M/M.

For Q1, net revenues for ATM assembly, testing, and material business were $3.57 billion, up 34.9% year over year and up 0.4% from last month.

data.title: Lufthansa to cancel over 500 flights amid UFO-led cabin crew strike
data.description:
Germany’s Deutsche Lufthansa  (DLKAF) is facing major travel disruption after a cabin crew strike called by the UFO union, with more than 500 flights expected to be canceled.

The one-day strike, scheduled for April 10, runs from 00:01 to 22:00 CET, primarily affecting departures from Frankfurt and Munich hubs, plus Lufthansa CityLine flights from nine German airports, including Hamburg, Berlin, and Düsseldorf.

The strike is expected to impact around 90,000 passengers returning home for the Easter holidays.

The UFO union, representing about 19,000 cabin crew, is demanding better working conditions, rosters, and a social plan for CityLine employees amid stalled talks.

“This situation could have been avoided—the responsibility lies with Lufthansa, which has not even managed to submit a negotiable offer so far,” explained head of UFO, Joachim Vázquez Bürger.

It marks the third major labor disruption lately for Lufthansa (DLAKF) amid ongoing disputes with unions.
